heading: 9.7.16.10.TensorCore 5th Generation Matrix Multiply and accumulate Operations (part 2)
instruction: TensorCore 5th Generation Matrix Multiply and accumulate Operations
content:
[Figure: tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf8f6f4-tmem-dig3]
6-BIT PACKING FORMAT WITH TYPE E2M3

SOURCE DATA TYPE:
- Type: E2M3 (floating point with 2 exponent bits and 3 mantissa bits)
- Bit width per element: 6 bits total (1 sign bit + 2 exponent bits + 3 mantissa bits)

CONTAINER:
- Container size: 8 bits (1 byte)
- The figure shows a single byte with bit positions labeled 7 down to 0 (left to right)

BIT-LEVEL LAYOUT WITHIN THE BYTE:

Bit position 7: 0 (padding, fixed zero)
Bit position 6: 0 (padding, fixed zero)
Bit position 5: S (sign bit of E2M3 element)
Bit position 4: E2 high bit (exponent bit 1)
Bit position 3: E2 low bit (exponent bit 0)
Bit position 2: M3 high bit (mantissa bit 2)
Bit position 1: M3 mid bit (mantissa bit 1)
Bit position 0: M3 low bit (mantissa bit 0)

FIELD SUMMARY TABLE:
Bits [7:6] = 00 (two padding bits, fixed zero, shown in white)
Bit  [5]   = S  (sign bit, shown in green)
Bits [4:3] = E2 (2-bit exponent, shown in blue/purple)
Bits [2:0] = M3 (3-bit mantissa, shown in yellow/tan)

PADDING:
- 2 padding bits at the most significant positions (bits 7 and 6)
- Both padding bits are explicitly shown as 0

PACKING DENSITY:
- One E2M3 element per byte
- 2 bits wasted per byte as zero padding
- Effective utilization: 6 of 8 bits

CONTEXT:
- This describes how a single 6-bit E2M3 value is stored/packed into an 8-bit byte container
- The element is right-justified with zero padding in the upper 2 bits


    Figure 201 6-bit packing format with type E2M3

###### 9.7.16.10.4.4. [Packing format used for matrix A and B by `.kind::mxf8f6f4` in Shared Memory](#tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf8f6f4-smem)

The 4-bit and 6-bit floating point elements in shared memory must be contiguously packed along
with padding as follows.

* 4-bit packing format as shown in [Figure 202](#tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf8f6f4-smem-dig1)

  

[Figure: tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf8f6f4-smem-dig1]
4-BIT PACKING FORMAT - COMPLETE EXTRACTION

FIGURE TITLE/CAPTION: "4-bit packing format"

---

1. SOURCE DATA TYPE AND BIT WIDTH

Data type: E2M1 (4-bit floating point)
Bit width per element: 4 bits
Format family: MX (Microscaling) floating point
Specific format name as used in instruction: mxf8f6f4 kind with E2M1 matrix element type

---

2. MEMORY TARGET

This format is for SHARED MEMORY (explicitly labeled "Shared Memory" on the left side of the figure).

---

3. PACKING STRUCTURE

Number of data elements per row/group: 16 elements of 4-bit floating point type
Total data bits for 16 elements: 16 x 4 = 64 bits
Padding appended: 64-bit padding added at the end of the 16 elements
Total container width: 64 bits (data) + 64 bits (padding) = 128 bits total per row

The shared memory row shown in the figure contains a wide horizontal strip of small cells. The first half (left portion) contains the 16 colored/shaded 4-bit elements, and the second half (right portion) consists of unshaded/white cells representing the 64-bit padding.

---

4. BIT POSITIONS OF ELEMENTS

Each element occupies 4 bits.
Element 0: bits [3:0]
Element 1: bits [7:4]
Element 2: bits [11:8]
Element 3: bits [15:12]
Element 4: bits [19:16]
Element 5: bits [23:20]
Element 6: bits [27:24]
Element 7: bits [31:28]
Element 8: bits [35:32]
Element 9: bits [39:36]
Element 10: bits [43:40]
Element 11: bits [47:44]
Element 12: bits [51:48]
Element 13: bits [55:52]
Element 14: bits [59:56]
Element 15: bits [63:60]

---

5. PADDING BITS AND POSITIONS

Padding type: 64-bit zero/unused padding
Padding position: bits [127:64] — immediately following the 16 data elements
Padding purpose: Alignment padding appended at the end of the 16-element group
The figure explicitly states: "16 elements of 4-bit floating point type, with 64-bit padding at the end"

---

6. INSTRUCTION CONTEXT

PTX instruction: tcgen05.mma.kind::mxf8f6f4
Matrix element type specified in instruction descriptor: E2M1
This packing format is consumed by the tcgen05.mma tensor core generation 5 matrix multiply-accumulate instruction operating on MX E2M1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa) 4-bit floating point data stored in shared memory.


  Figure 202 4-bit packing format
* 6-bit packing format as shown in [Figure 203](#tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf8f6f4-smem-dig2)

> 

[Figure: tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf8f6f4-smem-dig2]
6-BIT PACKING FORMAT - COMPLETE EXTRACTION

1. SOURCE DATA TYPE AND BIT WIDTH
   - Data type: 6-bit floating point
   - Supported formats: E3M2 (1 sign bit, 3 exponent bits, 2 mantissa bits) or E2M3 (1 sign bit, 2 exponent bits, 3 mantissa bits)
   - Each element is exactly 6 bits wide

2. CONTAINER AND ELEMENT COUNT
   - Memory type: Shared Memory
   - 16 elements of 6-bit floating point type are packed per group
   - 16 elements x 6 bits = 96 bits of actual data
   - 32-bit padding is appended at the end of the 16-element group
   - Total container size: 96 bits (data) + 32 bits (padding) = 128 bits (16 bytes) per group of 16 elements

3. BIT POSITIONS OF ELEMENTS
   - Elements are packed contiguously, back-to-back, with no gaps between individual elements
   - Element 0: bits 0-5
   - Element 1: bits 6-11
   - Element 2: bits 12-17
   - Element 3: bits 18-23
   - Element 4: bits 24-29
   - Element 5: bits 30-35
   - Element 6: bits 36-41
   - Element 7: bits 42-47
   - Element 8: bits 48-53
   - Element 9: bits 54-59
   - Element 10: bits 60-65
   - Element 11: bits 66-71
   - Element 12: bits 72-77
   - Element 13: bits 78-83
   - Element 14: bits 84-89
   - Element 15: bits 90-95

4. PADDING BITS AND POSITIONS
   - 32-bit padding appended at the END of the 16-element block
   - Padding occupies bits 96-127 (the final 32 bits of the 128-bit container)
   - Padding bits are shown as white/empty cells in the figure at the rightmost portion of the memory strip

5. MEMORY TYPE
   - This format is for SHARED MEMORY (explicitly labeled "Shared Memory" in the figure)
   - The instruction that consumes this format is: tcgen05.mma.kind::mxf8f6f4
   - The matrix element type (E3M2 or E2M3) is specified in the instruction descriptor
   - The kind qualifier mxf8f6f4 indicates this instruction handles mixed 8-bit, 6-bit, and 4-bit floating point formats

SUMMARY TABLE
Field                  Value
Data element width     6 bits
Elements per group     16
Data bits per group    96 bits
Padding per group      32 bits (at end)
Total group size       128 bits (16 bytes)
Packing style         Contiguous, no inter-element padding
Memory target         Shared Memory
Instruction           tcgen05.mma.kind::mxf8f6f4
Supported types       E3M2, E2M3

>
>
> Figure 203 6-bit packing format

###### 9.7.16.10.4.5. [Packing format used for matrix A by `.kind::mxf4` and `.kind::mxf4nvf4` in Tensor Memory](#tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf4-tmem)

Two 4-bit floating point type elements must be packed in an 8-bit container in Tensor memory as
shown in [Figure 204](#tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf4-tmem-dig1) for `mxf4`.



[Figure: tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf4-tmem-dig1]
4-BIT PACKING FORMAT WITH TYPE E2M1

SOURCE DATA TYPE:
- Type: E2M1 (floating point with 1 sign bit, 2 exponent bits, 1 mantissa bit)
- Bit width per element: 4 bits total (1 sign + 2 exponent + 1 mantissa)

CONTAINER:
- Container size: 8 bits (1 byte)
- Number of elements packed per byte: 2 elements

BIT-LEVEL LAYOUT WITHIN 8-BIT CONTAINER:
Bit positions are labeled 7 down to 0 (MSB to LSB).

Upper element (bits 7:4):
- Bit 7: S (sign bit)
- Bits 6-5: E2 (2 exponent bits)
- Bit 4: M1 (1 mantissa bit)

Lower element (bits 3:0):
- Bit 3: S (sign bit)
- Bits 2-1: E2 (2 exponent bits)
- Bit 0: M1 (1 mantissa bit)

ELEMENT FIELD WIDTHS:
- S field: 1 bit
- E2 field: 2 bits
- M1 field: 1 bit
- Total per element: 4 bits

PADDING:
- No padding bits present; all 8 bits are used by the two E2M1 elements

PACKING ORDER:
- Element 0 (lower) occupies bits 3:0
- Element 1 (upper) occupies bits 7:4
- Elements are packed contiguously with no gaps

CONTEXT:
- No explicit memory type (shared, tensor, or register) is labeled in the figure; the figure illustrates the generic byte-level packing format for E2M1 sub-byte data type


Figure 204 4-bit packing format with type E2M1

###### 9.7.16.10.4.6. [Packing format used for matrix A and B by `.kind::mxf4` and `.kind::mxf4nvf4` in Shared Memory](#tcgen05-packing-formats-mxf4-smem)

The packing format for 4-bit floating point elements in shared memory is to pack two 4-bit
elements in a 8-bit container, with no padding.

##### 9.7.16.10.5. [Data Path Layout Organization](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-organization)

Different MMA variants access the tensor memory with different layout organization.
The following table lists the various layouts:

| M | cta_group | A-Sparsity | Is .ws mode | Datapath organization | Layout ID | Tensor Memory Datapath Lane Alignment |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 32 | ::1 | Either | Yes | 1x4 | [Layout G](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-g) | 0 |
| 64 | ::1 | Either | Yes | 2x2 | [Layout E](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-e) | 0 |
| 64 | ::1 | Either | No | 4x1 (1/2 datapath utilized) | [Layout F](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-f) | 0 or 16 |
| 128 | ::1 | Either | Either | 4x1 | [Layout D](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-d) | 0 |
| 128 | ::2 | Dense | N/A | 2x2 | [Layout B](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-b) | 0 |
| 128 | ::2 | Sparse | N/A | 4x1 (1/2 datapath utilized) | [Layout C](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-c) | 0 or 16 |
| 256 | ::2 | Either | N/A | 4x1 | [Layout A](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-a) | 0 |

The layouts which utilize only half the datapath lanes, i.e.,
[Layout F](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-f) and
[Layout C](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-c), must use the same Tensor Memory
lane alignment across matrices `A`, `D` and the sparsity metadata matrix.

The following shows the warps that can access the Tensor Memory regions via
`tcgen05.ld` / `tcgen05.st` along with the addresses for various Tensor Memory Layouts.

###### 9.7.16.10.5.1. [Layout A (M = 256)](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-a)

Layout organization for M = 256 is shown in [Figure 205](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-a1).



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-a1]
LAYOUT ORGANIZATION FOR M = 256

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows a matrix multiplication data layout organization for a warp-cooperative matrix operation (likely wgmma or similar NVIDIA tensor core operation). It describes how rows of the A Matrix (with M=256 rows total) are distributed across warps and CTAs (Cooperative Thread Arrays) in a CTA-Pair configuration. The B Matrix spans columns 0 to N-1 and is shown as a single block across the top.

STRUCTURE:
The layout is a 2D matrix decomposition table with:
- Vertical axis: A Matrix rows (M dimension), divided into 8 row-groups of 32 rows each
- Horizontal axis: B Matrix columns (0 to N-1), shown as a single unified block

TABLE LAYOUT (8 rows x 2 columns):

Row 1: M 0-31     | warp-rank % 4 == 0  + Even CTA in CTA-Pair   [Dark green background]
Row 2: M 32-63    | warp-rank % 4 == 1  + Even CTA in CTA-Pair   [Medium green background]
Row 3: M 64-95    | warp-rank % 4 == 2  + Even CTA in CTA-Pair   [Light green background]
Row 4: M 96-127   | warp-rank % 4 == 3  + Even CTA in CTA-Pair   [Lightest green background]
Row 5: M 128-159  | warp-rank % 4 == 0  + Odd CTA in CTA-Pair    [Dark gold/orange background]
Row 6: M 160-191  | warp-rank % 4 == 1  + Odd CTA in CTA-Pair    [Medium gold/yellow background]
Row 7: M 192-223  | warp-rank % 4 == 2  + Odd CTA in CTA-Pair    [Light yellow background]
Row 8: M 224-255  | warp-rank % 4 == 3  + Odd CTA in CTA-Pair    [Lightest yellow background]

DIMENSIONS AND PARTITIONING:
- Total M dimension: 256 rows
- Each row-group covers exactly 32 rows
- 8 total row-groups
- 4 warp-rank groups (warp-rank % 4 == 0, 1, 2, 3)
- 2 CTA types in a CTA-Pair: Even CTA and Odd CTA

ASSIGNMENT PATTERN:
- Rows M 0-127 (first 128 rows) are assigned to the Even CTA in the CTA-Pair
- Rows M 128-255 (second 128 rows) are assigned to the Odd CTA in the CTA-Pair
- Within each 128-row half, the 4 groups of 32 rows are assigned to warps based on warp-rank modulo 4:
  - warp-rank % 4 == 0: first 32 rows of the half (M 0-31 or M 128-159)
  - warp-rank % 4 == 1: second 32 rows of the half (M 32-63 or M 160-191)
  - warp-rank % 4 == 2: third 32 rows of the half (M 64-95 or M 192-223)
  - warp-rank % 4 == 3: fourth 32 rows of the half (M 96-127 or M 224-255)

COLOR CODING:
- Green shades (4 gradations from dark to light): Even CTA in CTA-Pair (M 0-127)
- Gold/Yellow shades (4 gradations from dark to light): Odd CTA in CTA-Pair (M 128-255)
- Within each color family, darker shade = lower warp-rank value (0), lighter shade = higher warp-rank value (3)

LEFT LABEL: "A Matrix" labels the entire row structure
TOP LABEL: "B Matrix" with "0 to N-1" labels the column dimension


Figure 205 Layout organization for M = 256

Addresses for the above region to be used in `tcgen05.ld` / `tcgen05.st`
is shown in [Figure 206](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-a2)



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-a2]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED

This figure shows the memory address layout used for tcgen05.ld/tcgen05.st instructions (tensor core generation 5 load/store operations). It maps row ranges of the A Matrix to specific base addresses in memory, while the B Matrix spans columns 0 to N-1. This is a Tensor Memory Accelerator (TMA) or tensor core address mapping diagram showing how matrix tiles are addressed in the tcgen05 memory space.

OVERALL STRUCTURE

The figure consists of two labeled regions:
1. A header box labeled "B Matrix" with subtitle "0 to N-1" — this spans horizontally across the top, indicating the column dimension covers 0 to N-1.
2. A grid/table below labeled "A Matrix" on the left side — this shows 8 rows, each representing a range of M (row) indices and their corresponding memory addresses.

TABLE LAYOUT (8 rows, 2 columns)

Left column header (implicit): Row range of A Matrix (M indices)
Right column header (implicit): Base address for tcgen05.ld/tcgen05.st

Row 1: M 0-31      | 0x0000.0XXX   (dark green background)
Row 2: M 32-63     | 0x0020.0XXX   (medium green background)
Row 3: M 64-95     | 0x0040.0XXX   (light green background)
Row 4: M 96-127    | 0x0060.0XXX   (very light green background)
Row 5: M 128-159   | 0x0080.0XXX   (dark golden/amber background)
Row 6: M 160-191   | 0x00A0.0XXX   (medium yellow/gold background)
Row 7: M 192-223   | 0x00C0.0XXX   (light yellow background)
Row 8: M 224-255   | 0x00E0.0XXX   (very light yellow/cream background)

NUMERICAL VALUES AND DIMENSIONS

M row ranges: Each range spans 32 rows (0-31, 32-63, 64-95, 96-127, 128-159, 160-191, 192-223, 224-255), covering a total of 256 rows (M = 0 to 255).

Address spacing: Each successive address block is offset by 0x0020.0000 from the previous one:
  0x0000.0XXX
  0x0020.0XXX
  0x0040.0XXX
  0x0060.0XXX
  0x0080.0XXX
  0x00A0.0XXX
  0x00C0.0XXX
  0x00E0.0XXX

The "XXX" notation in the addresses indicates that the lower 12 bits (3 hex digits) are variable/offset bits used within each 32-row block, while the upper bits form the base address for that tile.

COLOR CODING

Rows 1-4 (M 0-127): Shades of green, darkest at top (M 0-31) transitioning to lightest green (M 96-127), indicating one group or tile set.
Rows 5-8 (M 128-255): Shades of yellow/amber/gold, darkest at M 128-159 transitioning to lightest cream at M 224-255, indicating a second group or tile set.

The color gradient within each group (green group and yellow group) likely indicates sub-groupings or visual distinction between the two halves of the 256-row M dimension.

DATA ORGANIZATION PATTERN

The A Matrix is partitioned into 8 tiles of 32 rows each. Each tile maps to a distinct base address region separated by 0x20 in the upper address bits. The B Matrix dimension (N) is not partitioned in this figure — it spans the full range 0 to N-1 as a single block. This suggests the addressing scheme partitions along the M dimension (rows of A Matrix) into 32-row chunks, each assigned a unique starting address for use with tcgen05 load/store instructions.


Figure 206 Addresses to use in `tcgen05.ld` / `tcgen05.st`

###### 9.7.16.10.5.2. [Layout B (M = 128 + cta-group::2 + Dense A matrix)](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-b)

Layout organization for M = 128 + .cta_group::2 + Dense A matrix is shown in
[Figure 207](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-b1).



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-b1]
LAYOUT ORGANIZATION: M = 128 + .cta_group::2 + Dense A Matrix

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows a 2D matrix layout mapping for a Dense A matrix with M=128 rows, used in a CTA-group configuration with 2 CTAs (cta_group::2). It defines which warps (identified by warp-rank) and which CTA (Even or Odd in a CTA-Pair) are responsible for each tile of the A matrix, across two halves of the B matrix (columns).

TOP HEADER ROW (B Matrix column partitioning):
- Left column header: "B Matrix, 0 to (N/2)-1"  [columns 0 through (N/2)-1]
- Right column header: "B Matrix, (N/2) to N-1"  [columns N/2 through N-1]

LEFT SIDE LABEL:
- "A Matrix" labels the entire row section on the left

A MATRIX ROW PARTITIONING (4 row bands, each 32 rows wide):
Row Band 1: M 0-31    (light blue/gray background for label)
Row Band 2: M 32-63   (light blue/gray background for label)
Row Band 3: M 64-95   (light blue/gray background for label)
Row Band 4: M 96-127  (light blue/gray background for label)

CELL ASSIGNMENTS (warp-rank and CTA assignment per tile):

Row M 0-31:
  - B columns 0 to (N/2)-1:    warp-rank % 4 == 0  +  Even CTA in CTA-Pair   [dark green background]
  - B columns (N/2) to N-1:    warp-rank % 4 == 2  +  Even CTA in CTA-Pair   [light green background]

Row M 32-63:
  - B columns 0 to (N/2)-1:    warp-rank % 4 == 1  +  Even CTA in CTA-Pair   [light green background]
  - B columns (N/2) to N-1:    warp-rank % 4 == 3  +  Even CTA in CTA-Pair   [light yellow/green background]

Row M 64-95:
  - B columns 0 to (N/2)-1:    warp-rank % 4 == 0  +  Odd CTA in CTA-Pair    [dark gold/orange background]
  - B columns (N/2) to N-1:    warp-rank % 4 == 2  +  Odd CTA in CTA-Pair    [light gold/yellow background]

Row M 96-127:
  - B columns 0 to (N/2)-1:    warp-rank % 4 == 1  +  Odd CTA in CTA-Pair    [light gold/yellow background]
  - B columns (N/2) to N-1:    warp-rank % 4 == 3  +  Odd CTA in CTA-Pair    [light yellow background]

COLOR CODING SUMMARY:
- Dark green: warp-rank % 4 == 0, Even CTA
- Medium/light green: warp-rank % 4 == 1 or 2, Even CTA
- Light yellow-green: warp-rank % 4 == 3, Even CTA
- Dark gold/orange: warp-rank % 4 == 0, Odd CTA
- Light gold/yellow: warp-rank % 4 == 1, 2, or 3, Odd CTA

KEY ORGANIZATIONAL PRINCIPLES:
1. M dimension (128 rows) is split into 4 bands of 32 rows each.
2. N dimension (columns) is split into 2 halves.
3. The 2-CTA group is split: Even CTA handles M rows 0-63; Odd CTA handles M rows 64-127.
4. Warp-rank modulo 4 determines which 32-row band and which N-half a warp covers.
5. Warp-rank % 4 values 0 and 1 cover the left B-column half; values 2 and 3 cover the right B-column half — interleaved across the row bands.
6. The pattern assigns warp-rank % 4 == 0 and 2 to even M-bands (0-31, 64-95) and warp-rank % 4 == 1 and 3 to odd M-bands (32-63, 96-127).


Figure 207 Layout organization for M = 128 + .cta_group::2 + Dense A matrix

Addresses for the above region to be used in `tcgen05.ld` / `tcgen05.st`
is shown in [Figure 208](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-b2)



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-b2]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure shows the address mapping for tcgen05.ld/tcgen05.st instructions in NVIDIA PTX ISA. It illustrates how memory addresses are organized when accessing matrix tiles stored in tensor core memory (tcgen05 memory), specifically showing the base addresses to use for different row ranges of the A Matrix combined with different column ranges of the B Matrix.

OVERALL STRUCTURE:
The figure is a 2D grid/table with:
- Rows representing A Matrix row ranges (M dimension)
- Columns representing B Matrix column ranges (N dimension)
- Each cell contains a hexadecimal base address with a variable low-order nibble (XXX)

COLUMN HEADERS (B Matrix dimension):
There are two columns, each labeled "B Matrix":
- Left column:  "B Matrix" / "0 to (N/2)-1"       (columns from 0 to N/2 minus 1)
- Right column: "B Matrix" / "(N/2) to N-1"        (columns from N/2 to N minus 1)

ROW LABELS (A Matrix dimension, labeled "A Matrix" on the left side):
There are four rows:
- Row 1: M 0-31      (rows 0 through 31)
- Row 2: M 32-63     (rows 32 through 63)
- Row 3: M 64-95     (rows 64 through 95)
- Row 4: M 96-127    (rows 96 through 127)

ADDRESS VALUES IN EACH CELL (row x column):

Row M 0-31:
  Left  (B cols 0 to N/2-1):   0x0000.0XXX   [dark green background]
  Right (B cols N/2 to N-1):   0x0040.0XXX   [light green background]

Row M 32-63:
  Left  (B cols 0 to N/2-1):   0x0020.0XXX   [light green background]
  Right (B cols N/2 to N-1):   0x0060.0XXX   [light green background]

Row M 64-95:
  Left  (B cols 0 to N/2-1):   0x0000.0XXX   [dark golden/brown background]
  Right (B cols N/2 to N-1):   0x0040.0XXX   [golden/yellow background]

Row M 96-127:
  Left  (B cols 0 to N/2-1):   0x0020.0XXX   [light yellow/cream background]
  Right (B cols N/2 to N-1):   0x0060.0XXX   [light yellow/cream background]

COLOR CODING PATTERN:
- Rows M 0-31 and M 32-63 use green shades (darker green for 0x0000.0XXX, lighter green for others)
- Rows M 64-95 and M 96-127 use yellow/golden shades (darker golden for 0x0000.0XXX, lighter yellow for others)
- The color grouping suggests that rows 0-63 share one color family and rows 64-127 share another, possibly indicating different memory banks or regions

ADDRESS PATTERN OBSERVATIONS:
- The XXX suffix indicates that the lower 12 bits (3 hex digits) are variable/offset-dependent
- The upper address bits follow a repeating pattern:
  - 0x0000.0XXX appears for (M 0-31, left) and (M 64-95, left) — same base address repeats every 64 rows
  - 0x0020.0XXX appears for (M 32-63, left) and (M 96-127, left)
  - 0x0040.0XXX appears for (M 0-31, right) and (M 64-95, right)
  - 0x0060.0XXX appears for (M 32-63, right) and (M 96-127, right)
- The right column (B cols N/2 to N-1) addresses are always 0x0040 higher than the corresponding left column addresses
- Row stride between M 0-31 and M 32-63 is 0x0020 in the address

DIMENSIONS COVERED:
- A Matrix M dimension: 128 rows total (0 to 127), split into 4 groups of 32
- B Matrix N dimension: N columns total, split into 2 halves


Figure 208 Addresses to use in `tcgen05.ld` / `tcgen05.st`

###### 9.7.16.10.5.3. [Layout C (M = 128 + cta-group::2 + Sparse A matrix)](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-c)

Layout organization for M = 128 + .cta_group::2 + Sparse A matrix is shown in
[Figure 209](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-c1).



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-c1]
LAYOUT ORGANIZATION: M = 128 + .cta_group::2 + Sparse A Matrix

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows the warp-rank and CTA (Cooperative Thread Array) assignment layout for a Sparse A matrix with M=128 rows, used in a matrix multiplication operation with a CTA group size of 2 (cta_group::2). The layout maps M-dimension row ranges to specific warp-ranks and CTA positions within a CTA-Pair.

MATRIX DIMENSIONS:
- A Matrix: 128 rows (M dimension, rows 0 through 127), columns not explicitly bounded
- B Matrix: columns 0 to N-1 (shown as a single block at the top right)

LAYOUT TABLE STRUCTURE:
The A Matrix is divided into 8 horizontal row bands, each spanning 16 rows. Each band is assigned to a specific warp-rank modulo condition and a CTA designation (Even or Odd within a CTA-Pair).

ROW BAND ASSIGNMENTS (top to bottom):

Row Band 1: M 0-15
  Color: Dark green
  Assignment: warp-rank % 4 == 0 + Even CTA in CTA-Pair

Row Band 2: M 16-31
  Color: Medium green
  Assignment: warp-rank % 4 == 1 + Even CTA in CTA-Pair

Row Band 3: M 32-47
  Color: Light green
  Assignment: warp-rank % 4 == 2 + Even CTA in CTA-Pair

Row Band 4: M 48-63
  Color: Pale/yellow-green
  Assignment: warp-rank % 4 == 3 + Even CTA in CTA-Pair

Row Band 5: M 64-79
  Color: Dark gold/orange-yellow
  Assignment: warp-rank % 4 == 0 + Odd CTA in CTA-Pair

Row Band 6: M 80-95
  Color: Medium yellow-orange
  Assignment: warp-rank % 4 == 1 + Odd CTA in CTA-Pair

Row Band 7: M 96-111
  Color: Light yellow
  Assignment: warp-rank % 4 == 2 + Odd CTA in CTA-Pair

Row Band 8: M 112-127
  Color: Pale yellow
  Assignment: warp-rank % 4 == 3 + Odd CTA in CTA-Pair

ORGANIZATIONAL PATTERN:
- The 128 M-dimension rows are split into two halves: rows 0-63 (Even CTA) and rows 64-127 (Odd CTA).
- Within each half, the 64 rows are further divided into 4 bands of 16 rows each.
- Each 16-row band is assigned to a warp-rank satisfying warp-rank % 4 == 0, 1, 2, or 3 respectively.
- The warp-rank modulo pattern repeats identically for both Even and Odd CTA halves.
- Color coding uses green shades for Even CTA rows and yellow/gold shades for Odd CTA rows, with intensity decreasing as warp-rank index increases within each group.

CTA-PAIR STRUCTURE:
- cta_group::2 means CTAs operate in pairs.
- The Even CTA in the pair handles M rows 0-63.
- The Odd CTA in the pair handles M rows 64-127.
- Each CTA uses 4 warp-ranks (0 through 3 mod 4) to cover its 64 rows, with each warp-rank covering 16 rows.

SUMMARY OF MAPPING:
warp-rank % 4 == 0, Even CTA -> M 0-15
warp-rank % 4 == 1, Even CTA -> M 16-31
warp-rank % 4 == 2, Even CTA -> M 32-47
warp-rank % 4 == 3, Even CTA -> M 48-63
warp-rank % 4 == 0, Odd CTA  -> M 64-79
warp-rank % 4 == 1, Odd CTA  -> M 80-95
warp-rank % 4 == 2, Odd CTA  -> M 96-111
warp-rank % 4 == 3, Odd CTA  -> M 112-127


Figure 209 Layout organization for M = 128 + .cta_group::2 + Sparse A matrix

is shown in [Figure 210](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-c2)



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-c2]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure shows the address mapping used for tcgen05.ld/tcgen05.st instructions in NVIDIA PTX ISA. It illustrates how addresses are organized for accessing a matrix stored in tensor core memory (tcgen05), specifically showing the relationship between row groups of the A Matrix and their corresponding base addresses, while the B Matrix spans columns 0 to N-1.

OVERALL STRUCTURE:
The figure is a 2D table/grid representing a matrix multiplication operand layout. The horizontal axis represents the B Matrix (columns 0 to N-1). The vertical axis (left side) represents the A Matrix, divided into row groups. The label "A Matrix" appears to the left of the table. The label "B Matrix" with "0 to N-1" appears above the table in a separate header box.

TABLE LAYOUT:
The table has 8 rows, each representing a group of 16 rows of the A Matrix. Each row has two columns: the left column shows the row range label, and the right column shows two hexadecimal address values separated by a pipe character (|).

ROW-BY-ROW DATA (left column = row range, right column = addresses):

Row 1: M 0-15       | 0x0000.0XXX | 0x0010.0XXXX   (dark green background)
Row 2: M 16-31      | 0x0020.0XXX | 0x0030.0XXXX   (medium green background)
Row 3: M 32-47      | 0x0040.0XXX | 0x0050.0XXXX   (light green background)
Row 4: M 48-63      | 0x0060.0XXX | 0x0070.0XXXX   (very light green background)
Row 5: M 64-79      | 0x0080.0XXX | 0x0090.0XXXX   (dark golden/amber background)
Row 6: M 80-95      | 0x00A0.0XXX | 0x00B0.0XXXX   (medium yellow/gold background)
Row 7: M 96-111     | 0x00C0.0XXX | 0x00D0.0XXXX   (light yellow background)
Row 8: M 112-127    | 0x00E0.0XXX | 0x00F0.0XXXX   (very light yellow/cream background)

COLOR CODING:
Rows 1-4 use shades of green (darkest at top, progressively lighter going down).
Rows 5-8 use shades of yellow/amber/gold (darkest at row 5, progressively lighter going down).
The left column (row range labels) uses a consistent light blue/gray background for all 8 rows.

ADDRESS PATTERN OBSERVATIONS:
Each row group of 16 M-rows has two associated base addresses.
The addresses increment by 0x0010 between each address within a row, and by 0x0020 between consecutive row groups.
The XXX portions of the addresses represent variable/offset bits that depend on the specific access within that row group.
The total address range spans from 0x0000.0XXX to 0x00F0.0XXXX, covering 16 address blocks of 0x0010 each.
Each pair of addresses per row group is separated by 0x0010.

NUMERICAL DIMENSIONS:
Total M rows covered: 128 (M 0 through M 127)
Each row group spans 16 rows.
Number of row groups: 8.
Address spacing between pairs within a group: 0x0010.
Address spacing between groups: 0x0020.

###### 9.7.16.10.5.4. [Layout D (M = 128 + cta-group::1)](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-d)

Layout organization for M = 128 + .cta_group::1 is shown in
[Figure 211](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-d1).



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-d1]
LAYOUT ORGANIZATION FOR M = 128 + .cta_group::1

OVERVIEW:
This figure illustrates the warp-rank assignment layout for a matrix multiplication operation where M = 128, using a single CTA group (cta_group::1). It shows how rows of the A Matrix are partitioned among warps based on warp-rank modulo 4, and how the B Matrix spans the full N dimension.

COMPONENTS:

B MATRIX (top section):
- Displayed as a single wide horizontal rectangle at the top
- Labeled "B Matrix"
- Spans columns labeled "0 to N-1"
- Represents the full column dimension N, undivided
- No warp partitioning shown for B Matrix in this figure

A MATRIX (bottom section):
- Displayed as a vertical stack of 4 horizontal rows
- Labeled "A Matrix" on the left side
- Total M dimension = 128 rows, divided into 4 equal partitions of 32 rows each
- Each partition is assigned to warps based on warp-rank modulo 4

ROW PARTITIONING OF A MATRIX:

Row 1 (top):    M rows 0-31    assigned to warps where warp-rank % 4 == 0   (darkest green)
Row 2:          M rows 32-63   assigned to warps where warp-rank % 4 == 1   (medium-dark green)
Row 3:          M rows 64-95   assigned to warps where warp-rank % 4 == 2   (medium-light green)
Row 4 (bottom): M rows 96-127  assigned to warps where warp-rank % 4 == 3   (lightest green)

PARTITIONING DETAILS:
- Each partition covers exactly 32 consecutive M-dimension rows
- 4 partitions total, covering M = 0 through M = 127 (128 rows total)
- Assignment rule: partition index i (0 through 3) is handled by warps satisfying warp-rank % 4 == i
- Color coding uses a gradient from darkest green (partition 0) to lightest green (partition 3), visually distinguishing the four groups

LAYOUT STRUCTURE:
- A Matrix is partitioned in the M (row) dimension only
- B Matrix is not partitioned; all warps access the full N dimension (columns 0 to N-1)
- The partitioning is row-major in the M dimension with contiguous 32-row blocks
- With cta_group::1, all warp assignments are within a single CTA group, so the modulo-4 warp-rank mapping covers all 4 partitions within one CTA group

RELATIONSHIP SUMMARY:
- M dimension (128 rows) divided into 4 equal chunks of 32 rows each
- Each chunk mapped to one warp-rank residue class modulo 4
- B Matrix remains fully shared across all warp ranks (no partitioning in N dimension shown)
- This layout ensures each warp rank computes a distinct 32-row horizontal slice of the output matrix


Figure 211 Layout organization for M = 128 + .cta_group::1

CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure shows the address layout used for tcgen05.ld/tcgen05.st instructions in NVIDIA PTX ISA. It illustrates how memory addresses are organized for accessing the A Matrix and B Matrix in tensor core generation 5 (tcgen05) load/store operations. The figure maps matrix row ranges to specific base addresses in memory.

OVERALL LAYOUT:
The figure consists of two main components arranged spatially to suggest a matrix multiplication layout:
1. A "B Matrix" box positioned in the upper right area
2. An "A Matrix" table positioned in the lower left area
These are arranged to visually suggest the relationship between A and B matrices in a matrix multiply-accumulate operation.

B MATRIX COMPONENT:
- Labeled "B Matrix" at the top of a rectangular box
- Subtitle inside the box reads "0 to N-1"
- This indicates the B matrix spans columns 0 through N-1
- The box is outlined with a thin border, white/unfilled background
- Positioned in the upper right of the figure

A MATRIX COMPONENT:
- Labeled "A Matrix" on the left side (outside the table)
- Consists of a 2-column table with 4 rows
- Left column (light blue background): contains row range labels
- Right column (green background, varying shades): contains hexadecimal addresses

A MATRIX TABLE - ROW-BY-ROW DATA:

Row 1 (darkest green on right):
  Left cell: M 0-31
  Right cell: 0x0000.0XXX

Row 2 (medium-dark green on right):
  Left cell: M 32-63
  Right cell: 0x0020.0XXX

Row 3 (medium-light green on right):
  Left cell: M 64-95
  Right cell: 0x0040.0XXX

Row 4 (lightest green on right):
  Left cell: M 96-127
  Right cell: 0x0060.0XXX

NUMERICAL VALUES AND ADDRESS PATTERN:
- The A matrix covers M rows 0 through 127, divided into 4 groups of 32 rows each
- Base addresses increment by 0x0020.0000 for each group of 32 rows:
  - Rows M 0-31:   base address 0x0000.0XXX
  - Rows M 32-63:  base address 0x0020.0XXX
  - Rows M 64-95:  base address 0x0040.0XXX
  - Rows M 96-127: base address 0x0060.0XXX
- The "XXX" suffix in each address indicates that the lower 12 bits (3 hex digits) are variable/offset bits determined by the specific element being accessed
- The stride between consecutive 32-row blocks is 0x00200000 (2 MB) in the upper address bits

COLOR CODING:
- Left column cells (row range labels): light blue background
- Right column cells (addresses): gradient of green shades from darkest (top row) to lightest (bottom row), visually distinguishing each address range

DATA ORGANIZATION PATTERN:
- The A matrix is partitioned along the M dimension (rows) into 32-row chunks
- Each 32-row chunk maps to a distinct base address region
- The total M dimension shown is 128 rows (0 to 127)
- The B matrix dimension is N (0 to N-1), with no address breakdown shown for B in this figure
- The address spacing of 0x0020.0000 between consecutive A matrix row groups indicates a fixed stride of 0x20000 * 16 = 2MB per 32-row block in the tcgen05 tensor memory layout

###### 9.7.16.10.5.5. [Layout E (M = 64 + .ws mode)](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-e)

Layout organization for M = 64 + .ws mode is shown in
[Figure 213](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-e1).



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-e1]
Layout Organization for M = 64 + .ws Mode

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows a matrix multiplication layout organization for warp-specialized (.ws) mode with M = 64. It depicts how the A Matrix rows and B Matrix columns are distributed across warp-ranks in a 2D grid arrangement.

STRUCTURE:

The figure consists of two sections:
1. A top row showing B Matrix column partitioning
2. A bottom grid showing A Matrix row partitioning with warp-rank assignments

B MATRIX (top row, two cells):
- Left cell (larger, bold border): "B Matrix" / "0 to (N/2)-1"
  - Covers columns from index 0 to (N/2)-1
- Right cell (smaller, lighter border): "B Matrix" / "(N/2) to N-1"
  - Covers columns from index (N/2) to N-1

A MATRIX (bottom section, labeled on left as "A Matrix"):
The A Matrix is organized as a 2-row by 3-column grid:

Column 1 (leftmost, blue/light blue shading):
- Row 1: "M 0-31"   — A Matrix rows 0 through 31
- Row 2: "M 32-63"  — A Matrix rows 32 through 63

Column 2 (middle, dark green shading):
- Row 1: "warp-rank % 4 == 0"
- Row 2: "warp-rank % 4 == 1"

Column 3 (rightmost, light green shading):
- Row 1: "warp-rank % 4 == 2"
- Row 2: "warp-rank % 4 == 3"

WARP-RANK ASSIGNMENT MAPPING:

A Matrix rows 0-31  + B Matrix columns 0 to (N/2)-1   → warp-rank % 4 == 0
A Matrix rows 0-31  + B Matrix columns (N/2) to N-1   → warp-rank % 4 == 2
A Matrix rows 32-63 + B Matrix columns 0 to (N/2)-1   → warp-rank % 4 == 1
A Matrix rows 32-63 + B Matrix columns (N/2) to N-1   → warp-rank % 4 == 3

ORGANIZATION PATTERN:
- M dimension (64 rows) is split into two halves: rows 0-31 and rows 32-63
- N dimension is split into two halves: columns 0 to (N/2)-1 and (N/2) to N-1
- Four warp-ranks (identified by warp-rank modulo 4: values 0, 1, 2, 3) are assigned to the four quadrants
- Warp-ranks 0 and 1 handle the left N half (columns 0 to (N/2)-1), for upper and lower M halves respectively
- Warp-ranks 2 and 3 handle the right N half (columns (N/2) to N-1), for upper and lower M halves respectively
- The assignment increments by 1 going down the M dimension (0→1, 2→3) and by 2 going across the N dimension (0→2, 1→3)


Figure 213 Layout organization for M = 64 + .ws mode

This figure shows the address mapping scheme for tcgen05.ld/tcgen05.st instructions in NVIDIA PTX ISA. It illustrates how memory addresses are organized when accessing A Matrix and B Matrix data in a tensor core generation 05 (tcgen05) operation, showing which base addresses correspond to which matrix partitions.

OVERALL LAYOUT:
The figure is organized as a 2D grid with:
- Columns representing B Matrix partitions (horizontal axis)
- Rows representing A Matrix row partitions (vertical axis)
- Each cell contains the address (or address prefix) to use for that combination

TOP SECTION - B MATRIX COLUMN HEADERS:
There are two B Matrix column regions shown at the top:
- Left column header: "B Matrix" / "0 to (N/2)-1"
  - This is a larger/wider box with a white/outlined border
- Right column header: "B Matrix" / "(N/2) to N-1"
  - This is a smaller box with a lighter appearance (no heavy border)
  - The text is smaller than the left header

LEFT SIDE - A MATRIX ROW LABELS:
The label "A Matrix" appears on the far left side, with two row subdivisions:
- Row 1: "M 0-31" (light blue cell)
- Row 2: "M 32-63" (light blue cell)

ADDRESS GRID (4 cells total, 2 rows x 2 columns):

Row 1 (M 0-31):
- Intersection with B Matrix columns 0 to (N/2)-1: 0x0000.0XXX (dark green background)
- Intersection with B Matrix columns (N/2) to N-1: 0x0000.0XXX (light green background)

Row 2 (M 32-63):
- Intersection with B Matrix columns 0 to (N/2)-1: 0x0020.0XXX (light green background)
- Intersection with B Matrix columns (N/2) to N-1: 0x0020.0XXX (light green background)

NUMERICAL VALUES AND ADDRESS PATTERNS:
- M 0-31 addresses: 0x0000.0XXX (base address 0x00000000, with lower bits variable, denoted XXX)
- M 32-63 addresses: 0x0020.0XXX (base address 0x00200000, with lower bits variable, denoted XXX)
- The offset between M 0-31 and M 32-63 is 0x0020.0000 (2 MB offset)
- The "XXX" notation indicates that the lower 12 bits (3 hex digits) are variable/offset-dependent

KEY OBSERVATIONS:
1. The B Matrix column partition (first half vs second half) does NOT change the base address — both B Matrix halves use the same address prefix for a given A Matrix row range.
2. The A Matrix row partition DOES change the base address — M 0-31 uses 0x0000.0XXX while M 32-63 uses 0x0020.0XXX.
3. The address stride between the two A Matrix row groups is 0x00200000.
4. The "XXX" suffix indicates the lower bits vary within each region.

COLOR CODING:
- Light blue: Row label cells (M 0-31, M 32-63)
- Dark green: Top-left address cell (M 0-31, B Matrix first half)
- Light green: Remaining three address cells

LAYOUT ORGANIZATION:

ADDRESS PATTERN ANALYSIS:
- Each row range of 16 M-rows maps to TWO addresses separated by a pipe character (|)

- The left column uses a consistent light blue background for all row labels

For tcgen05.ld/tcgen05.st operations, the A Matrix is divided into groups of 16 rows (M dimension), and each group of 16 rows is associated with two tcgen05 addresses. ###### 9.7.16.10.5.7. [Layout G (M = 32)](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-g)

Layout organization for M = 32 is shown in
[Figure 217](#tcgen05-data-path-layout-g1).



[Figure: tcgen05-data-path-layout-g1]
LAYOUT ORGANIZATION FOR M = 32

TYPE OF LAYOUT:
This figure shows a warp-level matrix fragment layout for a matrix multiply-accumulate (MMA) operation, specifically showing how the A Matrix (with M=32 rows) and B Matrix (with N columns) are partitioned across warp ranks. It is a horizontal strip/tile layout showing the correspondence between A Matrix row segments and B Matrix column segments, organized by warp rank.

OVERALL STRUCTURE:
The figure has two rows:
- Top row: B Matrix column range labels (4 segments)
- Bottom row: A Matrix layout with warp-rank assignments (4 segments plus a label cell)

TOP ROW - B MATRIX COLUMN PARTITIONING:
The B Matrix is divided into 4 equal segments along the N dimension:
- Segment 1: "B Matrix, 0 to (N/4)-1" — columns 0 through (N/4)-1
- Segment 2: "B Matrix, (N/4) to (N/2)-1" — columns N/4 through (N/2)-1
- Segment 3: "B Matrix, (N/2) to (N/4 * 3) -1" — columns N/2 through (N/4*3)-1
- Segment 4: "B Matrix, (N/4*3) to N-1" — columns (N/4*3) through N-1

Each B Matrix segment occupies equal width (N/4 columns each), and all four segments are displayed in white/unshaded boxes with black borders.

BOTTOM ROW - A MATRIX LAYOUT WITH WARP RANK ASSIGNMENTS:
The A Matrix row is shown as a horizontal bar with the following cells from left to right:

Cell 0 (leftmost, light blue/gray shading):
- Label: "M 0-31"
- This indicates the A Matrix covers rows M = 0 through 31 (all 32 rows, consistent with M=32)

Cell 1 (dark green shading):
- Label: "warp-rank % 4 == 0"
- Aligned directly below B Matrix segment "0 to (N/4)-1"

Cell 2 (medium green shading):
- Label: "warp-rank % 4 == 1"
- Aligned directly below B Matrix segment "(N/4) to (N/2)-1"

Cell 3 (light green shading):
- Label: "warp-rank % 4 == 2"
- Aligned directly below B Matrix segment "(N/2) to (N/4 * 3) -1"

Cell 4 (lightest green shading):
- Label: "warp-rank % 4 == 3"
- Aligned directly below B Matrix segment "(N/4*3) to N-1"

ALIGNMENT AND CORRESPONDENCE:
Each of the 4 warp-rank segments in the A Matrix row is vertically aligned with a corresponding B Matrix column segment:
- warp-rank % 4 == 0  corresponds to  B Matrix columns 0 to (N/4)-1
- warp-rank % 4 == 1  corresponds to  B Matrix columns (N/4) to (N/2)-1
- warp-rank % 4 == 2  corresponds to  B Matrix columns (N/2) to (N/4*3)-1
- warp-rank % 4 == 3  corresponds to  B Matrix columns (N/4*3) to N-1

DATA ORGANIZATION:
- The A Matrix (M=32) spans all 32 rows (M 0-31) and is replicated or shared across the full N dimension, but each warp (identified by warp-rank modulo 4) is responsible for a specific quarter of the N dimension.
- The B Matrix is partitioned into 4 equal quarters along the N dimension, each of width N/4.
- The layout is horizontal/row-major in presentation, with the N dimension running left to right.
- The warp rank modulo 4 determines which quarter of the N dimension a given warp processes.
- All 4 warp-rank groups cover the same A Matrix rows (M 0-31), but each operates on a different N/4 slice of the B Matrix.

VISUAL ENCODING:
- Color gradient from dark green (warp-rank % 4 == 0) to progressively lighter greens (warp-rank % 4 == 3) visually distinguishes the four warp groups.
- The "M 0-31" label cell uses a distinct light blue/gray color to differentiate it as a dimension label rather than a data partition.

OVERALL LAYOUT:
The figure has two rows. The top row shows B Matrix column range labels. The bottom row shows the A Matrix row label and the corresponding address values for each partition.

TOP ROW - B MATRIX COLUMN PARTITIONS:
There are 4 cells arranged horizontally, each labeled "B Matrix" with a column range:
- Cell 1: B Matrix, range "0 to (N/4)-1"
- Cell 2: B Matrix, range "(N/4) to (N/2)-1"
- Cell 3: B Matrix, range "(N/2) to (N/4 * 3) -1"
- Cell 4: B Matrix, range "(N/4*3) to N-1"

These 4 cells divide the N columns of the B Matrix into 4 equal quarters.

BOTTOM ROW - A MATRIX ROW AND ADDRESSES:
On the far left, outside the cells, is the label: "A Matrix"

The row contains 5 cells:
- Cell 1 (small, light blue/teal): labeled "M 0-31" — this represents the A Matrix row dimension covering rows 0 through 31.
- Cell 2 (dark green): contains the address pattern "0x0000.0XXX"
- Cell 3 (medium green): contains the address pattern "0x0000.0XXX"
- Cell 4 (lighter green): contains the address pattern "0x0000.0XXX"
- Cell 5 (lightest green): contains the address pattern "0x0000.0XXX"

The 4 green address cells align vertically with the 4 B Matrix column partition cells above them.

ADDRESS PATTERN:
All four address cells display the same pattern: "0x0000.0XXX"
The "XXX" portion indicates variable/offset bits within the address, while "0x0000.0" represents the fixed upper portion of the address. The four cells use progressively lighter shades of green (darkest to lightest, left to right), suggesting they may represent different address offsets or regions.

NUMERICAL VALUES AND DIMENSIONS:
- M 0-31: The A Matrix covers 32 rows (rows 0 through 31)
- N: The total number of columns in the B Matrix (parameterized)
- N/4: Each B Matrix partition covers one quarter of the total columns
- Four equal B Matrix partitions: 0 to N/4-1, N/4 to N/2-1, N/2 to 3N/4-1, 3N/4 to N-1
- Address format: 0x0000.0XXX (32-bit address with lower 12 bits variable)

DATA ORGANIZATION PATTERN:
The figure shows that for a given A Matrix row range (M 0-31), there are 4 corresponding address regions (one per B Matrix column partition), each with the base address pattern 0x0000.0XXX. The B Matrix is divided into 4 equal column partitions along the N dimension, and each partition has its own address space used with tcgen05.ld/tcgen05.st instructions.


Figure 218 Addresses to use in `tcgen05.ld` / `tcgen05.st`

##### 9.7.16.10.6. [Shared Memory Layout and Swizzling](#tcgen05-shared-memory-layout-swizzling)

If the bit `Transpose A Matrix` / `Transpose B Matrix` in the
[Instruction descriptor](#tcgen05-instruction-descriptor) is 0, then *K-major* is
used for matrix `A` / `B` respectively. If the bit `Transpose A Matrix` in the
[Instruction descriptor](#tcgen05-instruction-descriptor) is 1 then *M-major* is
used for matrix `A`. If the bit `Transpose B Matrix` in the
[Instruction descriptor](#tcgen05-instruction-descriptor) is 1, then *N-major* is
used for matrix `B`.

In a column-major default BLAS library such as cuBLAS, the matrices `A` and `B` with and
without transpose can be classified as either *K-Major* or *M-or-N-Major* as shown in the
following table:

|  | Non-Transposed | Transposed |
| --- | --- | --- |
| A | K-major | M-major |
| B | K-major | N-major |

To avoid confusion with `A`, `B`, `row-major`, `col-major`, `transpose`, and
`non-transpose`, we will use *MN-Major* and *K-Major* throughout this section.

The matrices in the shared memory are made up of one or more “swizzle layout atom”.
The exact layout of these swizzle atoms depends on the swizzling mode, swizzle-atomicity,
and the leading dimension. The layout of the swizzle are shown in
[Table 55](#tcgen05-smem-swizzle-mode)

Table 55 Layout for swizzle atoms
